docs.title: "Celery + Redis ingestion worker"
docs.summary: Document and URL ingestion run as Celery tasks against a Redis broker, not as inline FastAPI background tasks.

docs.title: "Observability stack: structured JSON logs, Sentry, request IDs"
docs.summary: Stdlib `logging` with a custom JSON formatter (NOT structlog), context vars carrying `request_id / tenant_id / user_id` into every log line, automatic redact...

docs.title: "Principal-based tenant isolation"
docs.summary: `Principal` is the immutable view of "who is making this request" derived from a verified JWT or API key.

docs.title: "Fixed-window rate limiting"
docs.summary: Rate limiting uses a **fixed-window** counter (not token-bucket), implemented as a Protocol with two backends: in-memory (single-replica dev) and Redis (mult...

docs.title: "SSRF defense for tenant-supplied URLs"
docs.summary: Tenants can hand us a URL we then fetch (URL ingestion of documents; outbound webhook delivery to a customer-supplied callback).

docs.title: "Stripe webhook idempotency via Postgres"
docs.summary: Stripe delivers webhook events at-least-once.

docs.title: "Decision: BlobStore handoff between API and Celery worker"

docs.title: "Decision: Vercel + Fly + Upstash + Supabase Storage deploy"

docs.title: "Decision: Postgres + MongoDB storage split (mid-migration state)"
docs.summary: The data model is split between Supabase Postgres and MongoDB Atlas, but the split is **partial and mid-migration**, not clean.

docs.title: "Decision: Pydantic AI over LangChain"
docs.summary: We chose Pydantic AI for the RAG agent instead of LangChain because it fits FastAPI naturally, has a far smaller surface area, and treats type safety as a fi...

docs.title: "Decision: Supabase Auth as primary, NextAuth retained as fallback"
docs.summary: The dashboard uses Supabase Auth (`@supabase/ssr`, email/password) and the FastAPI backend verifies the resulting JWT against the Supabase JWKS endpoint.

docs.title: "Feature: Conversation analytics dashboard"

docs.title: "Feature: API Key Management"
docs.summary: Customers create and revoke API keys for the embeddable widget and programmatic access.

docs.title: "Feature: Multi-tenant bot configuration"
docs.summary: Each tenant can define multiple chatbots, each with its own system prompt, model selection, document filter, and widget styling.

docs.title: "Feature: Document Ingestion"
docs.summary: The pipeline that turns customer-uploaded documents into searchable chunks.

docs.title: "Feature: Embeddable chat widget"
docs.summary: A self-contained JavaScript chat widget that customers embed on their own site with a single `<script>` tag.

docs.title: "Feature: RAG Agent"
docs.summary: The customer-facing chatbot.

docs.title: "Feature: RAG pipeline enhancements (rerank, query rewrite, citations)"

docs.title: "Feature: Stripe Billing"
docs.summary: Stripe-powered subscription billing for the MongoRAG SaaS.

docs.title: "Feature: Team management & RBAC"
docs.summary: Each tenant has multiple users with one of four roles: `owner / admin / member / viewer`.

docs.title: "Feature: Usage Metering and Rate Limiting"
docs.summary: Plan-tier usage metering and rate limiting for the MongoRAG SaaS.

docs.title: "Multi-Tenancy: Tenant Isolation"
docs.summary: MongoRAG runs all customers on a shared MongoDB cluster.
